findings.where: join payments p on o.order_id = p.order_id, then sum(o.order_total)
findings.rationale: Orders join payments 1-to-many (installments). After the fanout, each order row is duplicated per payment, so sum(o.order_total) over-counts total_revenue by the number of payments per order. Revenue must be aggregated at order grain before (or separately from) the payments join.

findings.where: join payments (INNER) following the left join orders
findings.rationale: The INNER join to payments silently drops orders with no payment row (and customers with no paid orders), contradicting the LEFT join intent and undercounting/omitting customers. Should be a LEFT join or restructured.

findings.where: sum(o.order_total) for customers with no orders
findings.rationale: Customers with no orders yield NULL total_revenue rather than 0. Coalesce for cleaner downstream semantics. Acceptable as SUGGESTION.

false_positive_traps.where: CTE naming (customers/orders/payments) and group by 1,2,3 ordinals
false_positive_traps.note: Conventional dbt style; must NOT be reported as a BUG.

notes: 2 must-catch data-correctness BUGs (fanout over-count, inner-join silent drop). Data-correctness must-catch recall required = 1.00.

hardGates.rationale: Swarm dispatched ≥2 isolated reviewers in parallel (not a solo pass).
Report classifies findings in the [BUG]/[SUGGESTION] format.
